corpus.title: Middle East: Oil surges as Gulf tensions escalate
corpus.signal_text:
Middle East: Oil surges as Gulf tensions escalate

Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels say they attacked two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea on Thursday, marking their first reported strike on a vessel since announcing a blockade of Saudi-linked shipping through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait earlier this week. The Houthis say the move is retaliation for Saudi Arabia’s blockade on Yemen and a recent strike on Sanaa’s international airport. US strikes Iran again to 'diminish threat to mariners, vessels' after attacks on Saudi oil tankers in Red Sea

US forces launched a fresh wave of strikes on Iran for the thirteenth consecutive day early on Friday after President Donald Trump vowed “major military punishment” for Tehran and its Houthi allies over attacks on two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea by the Yemeni rebels. Houthis Claim Attacks on Saudi Tankers In Red Sea, Raising Risk of New Chokepoint in Iran War

The Iranian-aligned Houthis announced military strikes on two Saudi oil tankers as part of a naval blockade, threatening global oil supply at the Red Sea while the U.S. military carried out ongoing strikes against Iran. The Houthis claimed missile and drone attacks on the Encelia and Layla tankers, raising concerns as multiple tankers rerouted to avoid the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.
